gaps.description: The Bill of Rights is an empty scaffold: all articles are marked 'To be defined by human principal'. The BoR is the constitutional identity and the source of enterprise scope, prohibited activities, and human authority reservation. Without a substantive BoR, Arbiter cannot perform meaningful scope queries, and the DIO lacks definitive boundaries for any operation.

Genome documents referenced by the MSP are absent. The genome directory is empty (none at seed). These documents are expected to contain organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier constraints that extend the MSP for fine-grained governance.

Governance-critical organs (Arbiter, Senate, Nomos) exhibit frequent state flapping between ALIVE and DEGRADED due to missed pongs. The snapshot shows multiple cycles of DEGRADED→ALIVE→DEGRADED within minutes, indicating potential instability that could disrupt constitutional processes like scope queries, amendments, and policy enforcement.

legacy.raw_response:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is an empty scaffold: all articles are marked 'To be defined by human principal'. The BoR is the constitutional identity and the source of enterprise scope, prohibited activities, and human authority reservation. Without a substantive BoR, Arbiter cannot perform meaningful scope queries, and the DIO lacks definitive boundaries for any operation.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP Purpose: 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules.' Also MSP Relationship to the Bill of Rights: 'The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights. Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.' BoR scaffold status confirms no human-authored content exists.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "All authorization gates are effectively permissive; the organism has no constitutional identity; governance branches (Arbiter, Nomos, Senate) operate on an empty foundation; human authority reservation is unenforced.",
      "category": "constitutional",
      "status": "open"
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "description": "Genome documents referenced by the MSP are absent. The genome directory is empty (none at seed). These documents are expected to contain organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier constraints that extend the MSP for fine-grained governance.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP Genome References: 'Genome documents live under `genome/` in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.'",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Organ-level governance lacks specificity; risk-tier controls are undefined; the MSP cannot apply differentiated rules to individual organs, leaving operational gaps.",
      "category": "documentation",
      "status": "open"
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-003",
      "description": "Governance-critical organs (Arbiter, Senate, Nomos) exhibit frequent state flapping between ALIVE and DEGRADED due to missed pongs. The snapshot shows multiple cycles of DEGRADED→ALIVE→DEGRADED within minutes, indicating potential instability that could disrupt constitutional processes like scope queries, amendments, and policy enforcement.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP Amendment Protocol requires Senate-Legislator and Senate-Rollout; MSP Purpose references Arbiter as the first compliance gate ('Arbiter scope gate (BoR perimeter)'). Consistent organ health is necessary for these gates to function reliably.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Amendments and scope adjudication may be delayed or fail during DEGRADED windows; the integrity of Nomos's dual-gate authorization could be compromised if organs are not continuously available.",
      "category": "infrastructure",
      "status": "open"
    }
  ]
}

raw_responses:
```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "gap_description": "The entire Bill of Rights is a scaffold with no authored articles. This means no relational principles—scope of enterprise, human authority reservation, interpretation rules, amendment protocol—are constitutionally defined. The DIO operates without a binding relational framework, so person-attention coverage (minder/hippocampus) has no authorized target to measure or align with. Every observation, commitment, or peer interaction model is constitutionally unbounded.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "section_or_article": "All Articles (I–VI)",
        "detail": "BoR version 1.0.0-seed contains only placeholder headings with '[To be defined by human principal]'. No substantive relational directives exist.",
        "text_snippet": "## Article I — Scope of Enterprise\n\n[To be defined by human principal]\n\n## Article II — Prohibited Activities\n\n[To be defined by human principal]\n\n## Article III — Core Identity\n\n[To be defined by human principal]\n\n## Article IV — Human Authority Reservation\n\n[To be defined by human principal]\n\n## Article V — Amendment Protocol\n\n[To be defined by human principal]\n\n## Article VI — Interpretation Rules\n\n[To be defined by human principal]"
      },
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 125,
      "person_attention_coverage_gap": "Current minder records deductive observations about Leon’s preferences (e.g., need for consultation before infrastructure changes, authority reservations) but there is no constitutionally authorized obligation to honor those preferences. Without BoR content, the DIO’s person-attention cannot verify whether it is constitutionally compliant—any peer model is floating, vulnerable to misinterpretation or disregard."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "gap_description": "The MSP contains zero substantive rules (bootstrap seed). No peer commitments, no institution-to-human relational constraints, no person-attention triggers are encoded. Consequently, Nomos cannot adjudicate whether an Action Proposal respects relational obligations to peers, and the rich deductive observations in minder cannot be converted into machine-actionable constraints.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section_or_article": "Rules",
        "detail": "The MSP seed explicitly states '(none)' under Rules. The DIO runs in default-permissive mode until Senate publishes rules, but relational rules are entirely absent.",
        "text_snippet": "> The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules via the standard PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout flow.\n\n_(none)_"
      },
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 64,
      "person_attention_coverage_gap": "The minder contains 208 observations about Leon and 253 about Claude, including many relating to authority, consultation, and trade-off preferences, but without MSP rules translating those profiles into governance constraints, the DIO’s person-attention system is purely documentary. It cannot enforce that Leon is consulted before infrastructure changes, nor can it block proposals that ignore his relational boundaries."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "gap_description": "BoR Article V (Amendment Protocol) is undefined. The MSP already specifies that a PEM classified as BOR_CONFLICT triggers Senate-Legislator to produce a BoR amendment proposal for the human principal. Without a defined amendment protocol, the DIO’s person-attention has no constitutional script for that critical human interaction: how to notify, how to obtain ratification, what constitutes valid acceptance, etc.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "section_or_article": "Article V — Amendment Protocol",
        "detail": "Article V is a placeholder with '[To be defined by human principal]'. No process for amending the BoR exists.",
        "text_snippet": "## Article V — Amendment Protocol\n\n[To be defined by human principal]"
      },
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 125,
      "person_attention_coverage_gap": "The minder observes Leon’s requirement to be asked before modifications, but it cannot model the specific flow where a BoR amendment is needed. Without an amendment protocol, person-attention cannot prepare a structured conversation, verify the human’s intent, or ensure the principal’s response (approval/rejection) is constitutionally recognized. The DIO may inadvertently bypass the principal or proceed with a BOR_CONFLICT resolution without proper relational grounding."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "gap_description": "MSP Amendment Protocol requires human approval via POST /amend/review with decision=approve. However, the BoR does not define the scope of human authority, making the constitutionality of this requirement ambiguous when the BoR is empty. Person-attention can follow the MSP literally but cannot know if the MSP’s requirement is itself constitutionally valid without a live BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation).",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP / BoR",
        "section_or_article": "MSP Amendment Protocol & BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation)",
        "detail": "MSP states: 'Activation requires a POST /amend/review with decision=approve.' BoR Article IV is empty, so no constitutional grant of human authority backs this mechanical requirement.",
        "text_snippet": "From MSP: 'Activation requires a POST /amend/review with decision=approve.' From BoR: '## Article IV — Human Authority Reservation\n\n[To be defined by human principal]'"
      },
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 64,
      "person_attention_coverage_gap": "The minder has deduced Leon’s preference to be consulted before infrastructure changes and his desire to see trade-offs. The MSP amendment protocol mechanic could honor that, but without a constitutional reservation of human authority, person-attention cannot guarantee that the human’s approval is the final and legitimate decision. The DIO might treat the approval as procedurally sufficient while lacking the constitutional mandate to defer entirely to the principal."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-005",
      "gap_description": "The MSP mandates a HOM notification to the human-principal mailbox on amendment review, but no rule (MSP or BoR) defines a person-attention obligation to monitor that mailbox, ensure delivery, or handle non-response. This operational gap means the DIO’s relational attention to the human channel is assumed but not verified.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section_or_article": "Amendment Protocol",
        "detail": "'The draft enters a review state and an HOM notification is emitted to the human-principal mailbox.' No subsequent rule covers acknowledgment, retry, or escalation if the human does not receive or act on the notification.",
        "text_snippet": "The draft enters a review state and an HOM notification is emitted to the human-principal mailbox."
      },
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority_score": 27,
      "person_attention_coverage_gap": "Current minder/hippocampus does not model the human-principal mailbox as an active peer with status checks. Observations are passive; there is no commitment to verify that the notification was delivered and understood. Person-attention cannot assert that the human has been effectively informed, risking that constitutional approval never occurs but the DIO proceeds anyway."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-006",
      "gap_description": "BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise) is empty. The DIO therefore cannot constitutionally determine which peers are inside the enterprise boundary. The minder currently tracks Leon (human) and Claude (persona), but without a defined scope, person-attention may misattribute relational obligations, ignore critical external stakeholders, or over-commit to personas that should not hold constitutional standing.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "section_or_article": "Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
        "detail": "Empty placeholder. No definition of which entities, agents, or people the DIO must attend to.",
        "text_snippet": "## Article I — Scope of Enterprise\n\n[To be defined by human principal]"
      },
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority_score": 36,
      "person_attention_coverage_gap": "The minder includes observations about Claude (a persona) as if Claude were a peer deserving relational commitments, but without a constitutional scope, the DIO may be building a person-attention model that treats a persona as an obligation-bearing peer when in fact the enterprise boundary should only include the human principal. Conversely, it may omit necessary relational targets. The coverage is unanchored."
    }
  ]
}
```
